_note: Manually curated 2026 Georgia election race data. Add one object per contested seat. Set activePhase to 'primary' or 'general' — flip it manually after primary results are confirmed. Populate general.candidates after the primary settles. See ga-elections-design.md for full schema documentation.
_incumbentIds: GA state member IDs (ocd-person/...) come from ga-members.json. Federal bioguideIds come from current-members.json. Find OCD IDs at openstates.org/ga/people/ — verify uncertain IDs against current-members.json before publishing.

Republican.name: Derek Dooley
Republican.bio: Born and raised in Athens, Georgia, Derek played football and graduated from Clarke Central High School. He attended the University of Virginia to pursue his undergraduate degree in Foreign Affairs and Government and chose to walk on to the UVA football team as a receiver, where he played all four years and was named first team Academic All-ACC his senior year. Derek then went on to earn his law degree from the University of Georgia in 1994.

Republican.name: Earl "Buddy" Carter
Republican.bio: Current U.S. Representative for Georgia's 1st Congressional District. Serving his 6th term in the US House of Representatives.

Republican.name: John Francis Coyne III
Republican.bio: CEO. Previously ran for U.S. Senate as a Democrat in 2016.

Republican.name: Jonathan McColumn
Republican.bio: Born in October 1964, Macon, GA, this little boy appears to have little chance for success in the USA. His surroundings reveal poverty and without a future except, he was born in the USA! The probability of achieving success in consonance with the message of pursuing one's own happiness may not reach him except, he was born in the USA! By the grace of God, through the graces of the offerings in the USA, coupled with his persistence to pursue and achieve excellence, he would become a Clergy, a Consultant, a University Instructor, and an U.S. Army General Officer.

Republican.name: Michael Collins
Republican.bio: Current U.S. Representative for Georgia's 10th Congressional District.

races._note: Open seat — Buddy Carter (R) vacated to run for U.S. Senate.

Democrat.name: Amanda Hollowell
Democrat.bio: Amanda Hollowell is a nationally recognized political strategist and the Chief of Campaigns at Color Of Change, the country's largest online racial justice organization. She brings decades of experience in organizing, advocacy, and leadership to the campaign trail, but her roots remain firmly planted in Georgia's 1st Congressional District.

Democrat.name: Defonsio Daniels
Democrat.bio: My life has been shaped by overcoming adversity, serving others, and never forgetting where I come from. I understand the struggles of working families, the hardships faced by rural and urban communities alike, and the sacrifices it takes to build a better future. My journey is rooted in hard work, resilience, and hope—values I hold dear.

Democrat.name: Joey Palimeno
Democrat.bio: My name is Joey Palimeno, and I've called Camden County, GA home for the past 9 years. I'm a working-class dad—not a career politician—who's tired of watching Washington forget the people it's supposed to serve.

Democrat.name: Joyce Marie Griggs
Democrat.bio: I served as a Deputy Director of a Military Intelligence programin Iraq. I retired as a decorated combat veteran. In war zones, no one is using talking points. I had to made life-and-death decisions for our troops and the Allied troops in the region... That level of responsibility shapes a person in ways very few people get to experience. Intelligence Officers are trained to see systems, consequences, and power clearly. One who always works, understanding that bad judgment or bad leadership can cost lives.

Democrat.name: Michael McCord
Democrat.bio: As a small-business owner, Michael McCord understands the struggles that working families face, especially in rural areas. His consulting work isn't about big corporate deals- it's about helping the local restaurant owner navigate supply chain costs or showing a family how to cut through insurance red tape. Last year, that hands-on approach saved his clients over $3.5 million.

Democrat.name: Patrick J. Wilver
Democrat.bio: Pat Wilver is a West Point graduate, an Army veteran, a small business owner, and an adventurer who walked the length of our great nation on the Pacific Crest Trail. These experiences have taken Pat across the nation and across the world, and deepened his belief that the founding promise of a more perfect union is worth fighting for. Traditional politics have failed working families. Pat is running to change that.

Democrat.name: Randall Jay Zurcher
Democrat.bio: This campaign is people-powered, and I'm committed to showing up in every corner of Georgia's 1st District—urban and rural, blue and red—because every voice deserves to be heard. If you care about your benefits, your rights, and your future—I'm talking to you. My opponent might dodge debates. I won't. I've spent years fighting for Savannah's educators, and I'll fight just as hard for you. Let's build a government that shows up—and stands up—for the people.

Democrat.name: Sharon Stokes Williamson
Democrat.bio: I'm running for Congress to bring common-sense, community-rooted leadership to Georgia's First District. After years in public health, education, and advocacy, I've seen how broken systems fail families, seniors, caregivers, and working people. My own family's struggles and my work with vulnerable adults showed me what's at stake when leaders don't listen.

Republican.name: Brian Allen Montgomery
Republican.bio: A battle tested conservative, Brian Montgomery is ready to bring his sense of duty and mission to Washington, fighting for the people of Southeast Georgia. He will stand shoulder to shoulder with President Donald Trump to put America First. Brian and his wife Carrie, a dedicated healthcare professional, have made Savannah their home for nearly a decade. They are raising two incredible children in a community they love and proudly serve.

Republican.name: Eugene Chin Yu
Republican.bio: A steadfast conservative and a loyal Republican since 1984, Eugene's political philosophy is firmly rooted in the Constitution. He has stood as a champion for the America First agenda, proudly securing the GOP nomination while running for the U.S. Senate in 2013 and in multiple campaigns for U.S. Congress. Complementing his vast real-world experience, Eugene also pursued additional continuing education training from Harvard Business School, ensuring his leadership strategies remain sharper and more effective than ever.

Republican.name: Jim Kingston
Republican.bio: My career hasn't been in politics. I built my life in the insurance industry, working with families, small businesses, and community leaders across the state. I've seen firsthand how federal red tape makes everything harder… for farmers trying to stay afloat, for small towns trying to grow, for businesses trying to hire, for families who are trying to chase the American Dream like I am.

Republican.name: Kandiss S. Taylor
Republican.bio: Kandiss has been a public school educator for 22 years serving as a 3rd grade teacher, school counselor, and administrator at the central office. Her job as Director of Student Services affords her the ability to help abused children, homeless, intervene with mental health on threats, assist in rehabilitation of delinquent youth, and work with families in crisis. She is a wife and mother of 3. Kandiss will tell you she loves Jesus with all of her heart, and He has called her to serve in public office. Her campaign slogan “Jesus, Guns, & Babies” has been quoted in all major news outlets as well as a hit on Amazon “The Boys.”

Republican.name: Dr. Krista Penn
Republican.bio: Dr. Krista Penn, a Navy veteran and healthcare executive, is a proud Georgian running as a Republican candidate for Congress in Georgia's 1st District. Her service in Afghanistan as a Navy nurse, and decades of caring for patients, have ignited her dedication to expanding healthcare access, advocating for veterans, promoting small businesses, and providing common-sense leadership. Krista is ready to fight for Southeast Georgia.

Republican.name: Pat Farrell
Republican.bio: Pat Farrell is an authentic American conservative. He owns and operates Farrell Farms — a thousand-acre cattle ranch producing corn, peanuts, cotton, and beef. Pat knows what it means to meet a payroll. To work the land. To build something real. Career politicians don't understand that. An early Trump supporter, Pat believes in putting America First. He's strongly pro-life, pro-Second Amendment, and committed to cutting government spending — starting with eliminating the Department of Education.

Republican.name: Benjamin Matthew Day
Republican.bio: Matt Day believes that if America is going to change, it must begin with Congress. He is deeply concerned that Washington has become a source of division, driven by political parties, wealthy donors, and elites. He believes Congress should be guided by common sense and service to all Americans — not partisanship or corruption. Matt is prepared to sacrifice everything — possessions, businesses, and material things — if that is what it takes to win this fight. For him, the greatest gift he can give his wife and children is not wealth, but a future of freedom — a future without fear of war, corruption, or collapse.

Democrat.name: George Melville Johnson
Democrat.bio: George Melville Johnson, Democrat for Congress, is running to change Washington now. George will advocate for women and families, return war powers to Congress, education, including reformation of the student loan system, senior protections, support of our rural communities, judicial reform, and has a vision for America regarding immigration, workers, a and to return jobs back to America through business improvement.

Democrat.name: Maura Kathleen Keller
Democrat.bio: Maura Keller stands for service, dedication, and progress. Born in South Weymouth, Massachusetts, and residing in Fayetteville, Georgia for 30 years, Maura's life has been a testament to the power of hard work and helping others. She and her husband, David, share 4 children, 2 grandchildren, and 2 dogs. Maura's story is not just of personal achievement but also of a steadfast dedication to serving her country and her community.

Democrat.name: Ansel Postell
Democrat.bio: Ansel Postell is a dedicated public servant, community advocate, and proud Georgian running to represent Georgia’s 4th Congressional District in the United States Congress. With deep roots in DeKalb, Gwinnett, and Rockdale counties, Ansel has spent his life listening to the concerns of everyday people and working to create solutions that strengthen families and communities. He believes government works best when it listens first, acts with integrity, and puts people over politics.

Democrat.name: Ben Truman
Democrat.bio: In 2016, Ben served as a volunteer for Hillary for America as well as the Jim Barksdale campaign. In 2017, he served as a volunteer for Ceasar Mitchell's mayoral campaign. Subsequently in 2018 and 2022, Ben donated his time to the unsuccessful gubernatorial bids of Stacey Abrams. During the winter of 2022, Ben was appointed as a Community Councilman for District 1 by Commissioner Robert Patrick. Finally in 2024, Ben engaged in community outreach for Vice President Kamala Harris's campaign.

Republican.name: James Raymond Duffie
Republican.bio: Running for election to Georgia's 4th Congressional District.

Democrat.name: Arnetress Elaine Beatty
Democrat.bio: N/A

Republican.name: John Oscar Salvesen II
Republican.bio: John Salvesen was born in Burbank, California. He earned a graduate degree from Santa Monica College in 1989. Salvesen's career experience includes working in the construction industry.

Republican.name: Justin Lee Pinker
Republican.bio: Justin Pinker grew up right here in Georgia's 6th District. His childhood wasn't defined by privilege - it was shaped by parents who worked multiple jobs to keep food on the table and a father who taught him that faith without action is empty.

Republican.name: Kevin Eugene Martin
Republican.bio: Kevin Martin is a lifetime resident of Georgia's 6th District. He is running as a Republican Candidate with Conservative Christian Values built on common sense and care for the people of the 6th District.  As a political candidate, Kevin Martin is for the People of the 6th District.

Democrat.name: Case Norton
Democrat.bio: A union working-class husband and father of 2 here in GA's 7th District, and I'm running for Congress because working families in North Georgia deserve a representative who shows up, listens, and understands the struggles many of us face every day.

Democrat.name: Jayson Toweh
Democrat.bio: Jayson Toweh comes from generations of service members, union leaders, and farmers — families who believed in hard work, responsibility, and leaving their communities better than they found them. That spirit shaped his life and career in public health, environmental quality, and economic stability.

Democrat.name: Larry D. Long
Democrat.bio: Larry Long is running for U.S. House of Representatives, Georgia District 7, because he's seen—up close—what happens when reckless leadership breaks government on purpose. For more than 21 years, Larry worked inside the federal government as a scientist and hydrologist at the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA). His job wasn't political. It was practical: protecting water, land, and public systems that working Americans rely on every day—and making sure taxpayer dollars were used responsibly.

Democrat.name: Tony Kozycki
Democrat.bio: Raised in LaGrange, Georgia, he learned early the values of discipline, accountability, and hard work as a student-athlete. Those lessons carried with him to the United States Naval Academy, where he was nominated by Senator Saxby Chambliss and trained to lead with integrity under pressure.

Democrat.name: Justin M. Lucas
Democrat.bio: N/A

Democrat.name: Kelly Esti
Democrat.bio: Our federal government is facing challenges unlike anything we've seen in our lifetime. These crises didn't appear out of nowhere — they are the result of YEARS of neglect, political games, and leaders who stopped listening to the people they swore to represent. Frustrated... Overlooked... or DOWNRIGHT WRONGED by your government are just SOME of the things you may feel. I hear you, and as a resident of GA and citizen of the United States... I feel that frustration alongside you. It is not too late to make things right!

Republican.name: Joel Gregory Poole
Republican.bio: In Congress, Gregg will stand with President Trump to secure the border, defend life, protect the unborn, crush the woke agenda, and protect the God-given rights that make America great. He answers to the people of Georgia's 9th District, not to lobbyists or the political elite.

Republican.name: Warren Samuel Couvillon
Republican.bio: As our conservative voice in Congress, Sam will stand shoulder to shoulder with President Trump to secure the border and stop the flow of illegal aliens into our country, tackle inflation and the ever-increasing cost of living, fight for the renewal of the Trump tax cuts, and stand with our farmers and small businesses.

Democrat.name: Caitlyn Gegen
Democrat.bio: Caitlyn Gegen is a proud 'Double Dawg' holding a bachelor's and master's from the University of Georgia as well as a master's from the London School of Economics. As a community organizer, she fought for women's rights and criminal justice reform. She is running because she is committed to fighting for working families, protecting human rights, and building a stronger future for Georgia's 9th District.

Democrat.name: Nick Alex
Democrat.bio: He married his high school sweetheart, Debbi, and together they raised two wonderful daughters. Today, they're the proud grandparents of four energetic grandsons who keep them inspired and grounded. They've called Georgia home for over 30 years, spending many of those years in the Atlanta metropolitan area. Five years ago, Debbi and Nick made the move to North Georgia to be closer to family and enjoy the beauty and community of the region. They now live in Rabun County, where they found a deep sense of belonging and purpose.

races._note: Open seat — Michael Collins (R) vacated to run for U.S. Senate.

Republican.name: Houston Gaines
Republican.bio: In the State House, Houston has been a champion for conservative policies and helped oversee efforts to maintain the Republican Majority. He was named James Magazine's Top Lawmaker last year. He's been awarded by the American Conservative Union Foundation (ACUF) for conservative achievement and has a 100% voting record with the Faith & Freedom Coalition and an A ranking with the NRA. In 2022, he was named one of the University of Georgia's 40 Under 40. While at UGA, Houston served as student body president.

Republican.name: Jeffrey Dwayne Baker
Republican.bio: Jeff Baker has signed the U.S. Term Limits Amendment Pledge, committing that as a member of Congress, he will cosponsor and vote for the U.S. Term Limits Amendment of three (3) two-year House terms and two (2) six-year Senate terms — and no longer limit. Career politicians have had their chance. Jeff believes in serving the people — not building a lifetime career in Washington. This pledge is his commitment to real accountability.

Republican.name: Ryan Christopher Millsap
Republican.bio: Ryan Millsap is stepping up as a political outsider and a proud MAGA warrior to smash the status quo and fight back against the radical left and the RINOs who have lost their way. He's running to defend the Constitution, protect American families, restore common sense, and stand with President Trump to put America — and Georgia's 10th Congressional District — first.

Democrat.name: Alexandra "Lexy" Doherty
Democrat.bio: Lexy grew up working with horses and learning firsthand that listening to the needs of the individual and showing accountability were the ways to build lasting relationships built on mutual respect and trust. Lexy had her first job at ten as a working student at a barn, learning the value of hard work. Her mom was a small business owner but lost everything when tourism dried up following the 2008 recession. Lexy watched as a hub of her local community made up of small family-owned businesses turned into corporate storefronts. Lexy learned how important supporting rural economies is when she watched her mother get forced out of her business before eventually working in a steel mill.

Democrat.name: John B. Dority
Democrat.bio: Running for election to the U.S. House to represent Georgia's 10th Congressional District.

Democrat.name: Pamela "Pam" Delancy
Democrat.bio: For more than 20 years, McDonough and Henry County, Georgia have been home. My husband and I raised our daughters here; both were cheerleaders at Henry County High School and proudly graduated. Our oldest child, our son, now lives in Kentucky. Throughout my military service, I remained deeply engaged in the community—volunteering with Hands of Hope and A Friend's House alongside my daughters. More recently, I have volunteered with Reaping the Harvest Outreach Ministries Food Bank, continuing my commitment to serving those in need. I am also a proud member of Shiloh Baptist Church and American Legion Post 516.

races._note: Open seat — Barry Loudermilk (R) did not file for re-election.

Republican.name: Chris Mora
Republican.bio: I stand firmly against the corruption and corrosion of Washington, D.C. I believe in voting according to our Constitution, and I aspire to be your voice; a representative you can trust and take pride in, ensuring that your concerns resonate loudly. With a servant's heart and a deep love for this country, I believe Georgia deserves leaders who treat public service as a patriotic duty, not a political career. It's time to put the country over self, restore trust in our institutions, and honor the values that make America strong.

Republican.name: John A. Cowan Jr.
Republican.bio: Dr. John Cowan is a neurosurgeon, a small business owner, and a lifelong conservative with ties to the 11th District that go back generations. His family has been part of the Bartow County community since the 1930s — his father serving the community as a physician and his mother as a career schoolteacher. He grew up on his family farm, graduated from Cartersville High School, and has spent decades caring for patients across Georgia's 11th District. Beyond the operating room, Dr. Cowan has built and led small businesses that create jobs and serve families throughout the district. John and his wife, Dr. Anne Cowan, are raising four children and are active members of their church, where John serves as an elder.

Republican.name: John Hobbs
Republican.bio: I know the struggles of the people I want to serve because I've lived them. My time volunteering in Brazil showed me the hopes and challenges of immigrant families. My blue-collar roots give me insight into the daily grind of working and middle-class Georgians. And as the spouse of a U.S. Army veteran, I've seen the sacrifices our service members make and the support they deserve when they return home. Now, I work as a machinist right here in Georgia's 11th District. I live the same working-class life as my neighbors, and I'm ready to take that perspective to Washington.

Republican.name: Lisa Anne Carlquist
Republican.bio: Lisa is running for Congress to fight for secure elections and restore trust in our voting system. She believes every legal vote must count—and that weak enforcement, loopholes, and political interference undermine confidence and invite abuse. She is also committed to workforce development and a skilled trade enhancement act. Lisa supports stronger border security, tougher penalties for traffickers, and giving law enforcement the tools they need to shut these criminal networks down.

Republican.name: Robert Allen Adkerson
Republican.bio: Although a conservative by nature, he realized that even if citizens don't pay attention to politics - politics pays attention to citizens. This compelled him to begin a journey of self-education and activism. He founded one of the first Tea Party groups in the state, and participated in several national rallies to protest the bad policies coming out of Washington. He also began to get involved with his local, district, and state Republican parties.

Republican.name: Tricia R. Pridemore
Republican.bio: Current Public Service Commissioner Member in Georgia. Tricia is running for Congress to advance the America First agenda alongside President Trump. She's someone who will secure our border, cut wasteful spending, defend our Second Amendment, protect life, and never back down when it matters most. President Trump needs reinforcements in Washington. Help is on the way.

Republican.name: William Brown
Republican.bio: Attorney.

Democrat.name: Barry Wolfert
Democrat.bio: Barry isn't a career politician, he knows what it means to show up, work hard, follow the rules, and still feel like the system is working against you. Barry is running for Congress to restore common-sense leadership in Washington. He will protect Social Security and Medicare, expand access to affordable health care, and ensure working and middle class families have a fair shot and opportunity at the American dream.

Democrat.name: Chris Harden
Democrat.bio: Your representative should be honest, accountable, and empathetic. Voters deserve leaders who will tell them the truth, take responsibility for their decisions, and listen to the people they represent. I will be that representative for my District 11 constituents but I need your vote first.

Republican.name: Tori Brett Branum
Republican.bio: After leaving the military, Tori became a successful Georgia entrepreneur, founding a women-focused firearms training company that equipped more than 13,000 women with the skills to protect themselves and their families. Her mission was simple: empower women, strengthen communities, and defend constitutional freedoms. Her leadership quickly made her a trusted voice statewide on safety, security, and self-reliance

Democrat.name: Brianna Woodson
Democrat.bio: My life is proof that no setback is final and no story is too broken to be rewritten. After overcoming addiction and rebuilding my life from the ground up, I made a promise: to use my experience to open doors for others who've been counted out, overlooked, or underestimated. I know what it's like to fight through hard seasons, to start over, and to turn pain into purpose. That's why I'm passionate about helping others do the same, not through empty promises, but through real policy, practical support, and leadership that understands the struggle. For years, I've served my community, connecting people to resources, mentoring those on their own paths to recovery, and building programs that create second chances. My work has always been about impact, not image.

Democrat.name: Ceretta Smith
Democrat.bio: I am Ceretta Smith. I've dedicated my life to service, grounded in vision, and have fought hard for my constituents, with joy. From serving nearly a decade as a U.S. Army medic to fighting for working families as a federal employee, and national union leader, I've always believed leadership means showing up for people, not special interests. For twenty-five years, I stood on the front lines for labor, civil, and human rights because every Georgian deserves dignity, fair pay, and opportunity, no matter their ZIP code. My vision for our future is rooted in that same belief: that government should work for people, not the other way around.

Democrat.name: Chris Stephens
Democrat.bio: Born and raised in Loris, South Carolina to hard working blue collar parents, wonderful providers for my sister and I. My father, a diesel mechanic, my mother a factory worker with dreams of becoming a nurse, a dream we're all proud she achieved. Like so many in our district, I grew up in a working-class household that taught me the value of hard work, fairness, and treating people with dignity.

Democrat.name: Tracell Peace-Nichols
Democrat.bio: Ever since I can remember, I have not been good at recognizing problems or issues without trying to find the best solution to fix them, which is why I am running for Congress! In addition, I am running because I will be an authentic, LOUD voice for our communities and our districts, with particular significance for Georgia's 12th district, projecting the interests I've heard from you! We all face some common interests and problems (the rising cost of food, gas, housing, healthcare), many same uncertainties reflected in the media reports, or the unforeseen challenges in the near future that make it too easy for me to say what I will do; however, I need to hear from more of you before we prosecute each problem together!

Democrat.name: Traci George
Democrat.bio: Traci George is a Gold Star family member, a mother of three, and a leader shaped by service, sacrifice, and responsibility. Her husband served this country, and her family paid the ultimate price. Traci knows that honor cannot stop at ceremony—it must be reflected in how government shows up for families long after the speeches are over. That lived experience defines her mission: to bring our respect H.O.M.E. For Traci, 'home' is not a slogan. It is what families are supposed to feel—safety, dignity, opportunity, and trust that the systems meant to serve them actually work. As a mother raising children shaped by sacrifice, she understands both the human cost of government failure and the discipline required to fix it.

races._note: Open seat — Incumbent David Scott (D) passed away in April 2026.

Democrat.name: Emanuel D. Jones
Democrat.bio: Emanuel D. Jones has dedicated nearly two decades to serving the people of Georgia in the State Senate, where he has been a steadfast advocate for economic development, education, social justice, and public safety. Since taking office in 2005, he has championed legislation focused on criminal justice reform, voting rights, and economic empowerment for underserved communities.

Democrat.name: Dr. Everton Blair
Democrat.bio: Educator and lifelong resident of Georgia's 13th Congressional District.

Democrat.name: Dr. Heavenly Elaine Kimes
Democrat.bio: As a small business CEO, I learned that a successful practice is about more than just a business—it's also about meeting payroll, creating jobs, and building a stronger community.

Democrat.name: Dr. Jasmine Clark
Democrat.bio: A mom, microbiologist, and State Representative who found her passion for science and helping others at a young age.

Democrat.name: Jeffree Monroe Fauntleroy
Democrat.bio: Serving more than 25 years in law enforcement. During that time, he developed a strong belief in building community trust and the importance of accessible mental health and counseling services.

Democrat.name: Joe N. Lester
Democrat.bio: You, too, have faced adversity. You have overcome challenges. Now is the time for all of us to come together—in hope, unity, and determination—to defend our democracy and achieve the unbelievable.

Republican.name: Jonathan James Chavez
Republican.bio: A 23-year veteran of the U.S. Air Force, Jonathan served with distinction, including deployments to Iraq, where he earned a commendation medal and was lauded for excellence by both the Sergeant Major of the Army for Iraq forces and the Surgeon General of the Air Force.

races._note: Open seat — Marjorie Taylor Greene (R) did not file for re-election.

Republican.name: Brian C. Stover
Republican.bio: Brian Stover is a lifelong resident of Paulding County, a husband of 17 years to his wife Rachel, a proud father of three, and staunch supporter of President Trump. He is a conservative businessman who understands firsthand what it takes to create jobs, meet payroll, and keep a business running in a challenging economy.

Republican.name: Clay Fuller
Republican.bio: He has argued criminal cases before the Supreme Court of Georgia and the Court of Appeals of Georgia. Clay is also a Lt Col and a Deputy Staff Judge Advocate in the Air National Guard, where he has been rated the #1 mid-career officer by his Wing leadership, and has earned the Air Force Commendation and Meritorious Service Medals.

Republican.name: Colton Moore
Republican.bio: My name is Colton Moore, Fmr. State Senator from Northwest Georgia, and President Trump's #1 fighter in the state. I'm running for Congress in Georgia's 14th District (GA-14) to lower taxes and costs, deport all illegals, and fight for the hardworking people of Georgia. For years, I've been ranked as Georgia's #1 most conservative Senator and have been recognized as “Trump's Floor Leader.

Republican.name: Eric Brad Cunningham
Republican.bio: The political landscape is often cluttered with complex jargon and partisan bickering. Many citizens feel disconnected from their representatives, leading to a growing sense of frustration. Eric Cunningham recognizes this disconnect and aims to bridge the gap by focusing on common sense solutions that resonate with the needs of the people.

Republican.name: Nicky Lama
Republican.bio: Nicky Lama is a fifth-generation Dalton business owner and lifelong Northwest Georgian whose roots run deep in the small-business, agricultural, and civic life of Whitfield County.Born and raised in Dalton, Nicky grew up helping his family build and operate local small businesses—his father as a general contractor and owner of Dalton Stamp & Coin, his mother as the former owner of Paper Princess (custom stationery and gift store), and his grandmother as the owner of Fast Foto.​

Republican.name: Reagan Christane Box
Republican.bio: She does not have a political history, but says she has worn many hats in her life, including studying art and history in Paris, performing shark and turtle research, and missionary work. Over the past 16 years, she has traveled the U.S. working with horses and helping ranchers.

Republican.name: Star Black
Republican.bio: A 28-year retired veteran of the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA), Star worked on the front lines of disaster recovery with local, county, and state governments, nonprofits, and private partners to deliver resources and relief in the aftermath of disasters. Her job was to bring logic to chaos, solve problems under pressure, and be a good steward of taxpayer dollars. I focused on solutions every day. Public service is about showing up when times are tough, not creating chaos for personal attention. In Congress, I will listen to your concerns, fight for your needs, and put the spotlight where it belongs — on the people of the 14th District.

Republican.name: Thomas J. Gray
Republican.bio: Tom is running for Congress because Georgia's 14th District deserves principled representation not political theater. From Dade, Walker, Catoosa, and Chattooga Counties to Whitfield, Murray, Floyd, Polk, Paulding, and Northwest Cobb, Tom is committed to listening, leading, and delivering results. He understands the region's infrastructure, transportation, and economic needs and believes engaged leadership can make a real difference. Tom is especially committed to strong constituent services that treat every call and concern with urgency and respect.

Republican.name: Timothy Beau Brown
Republican.bio: MY AGENDA IS AMERICA FIRST ANO FOREMOST! MTG SERVED OUR DISTRICT INCREDIBLY WELL AND WITH HER SET TO RESIGN IT IS NOW TIME FOR ME TO CARRY THE TORCH. YES. THE GOVERNMENT HAS ITS PLACE. BUT I BELIEVE IN THE FREE MARKET. I BELIEVE VETERANS DESERVE THE BEST! MONEY IN WASHINGTON MUST BE REPRIORITIZED TO SERVE THE AMERICAN TAXPAYER. AMERICANS SHOULD KEEP MORE OF THEIR HARD EARNED MONEY! NO LONGER CAN WE LET THE MIDDLE CLASS BE CRUSHED BY RISING COST. UNFETTERED GOVERNMENT SPENDING THAT DOES NOT BENEFIT THE AMERICAN TAXPAYER. I Will FIGHT TO COMBAT AFFORDABILITY AND THE RISING COST OF LIVING. I WILL WORK TO SECURE FUNDING FOR INFRASTRUCTURE SPENDING TO TACKLE WORN ROADWAYS. 70-YEAR-OLD. BRIDGES AND INSTALLING UNDERGROUND POWER THROUGHOUT NWGA. AMERICANS ARE STRETCHED THIN WITH INCREASING POWER BILLS. HOME AFFORDABILITY. ANO CHILDCARE COSTS. THERE IS NOTHING LEFT TO INVEST IN THEIR OWN AMERICAN DREAM. THIS MUST CHANGE!

Democrat.name: Shawn Andre Harris
Democrat.bio: I grew up on a Georgia farm, and I enlisted in the Marines to serve my country. My wife Karla and I have been married for 35 years. She became a family doctor while I served, and we raised 5 kids and have 4 grandkids. After spending 40 years in the military, including serving as a combat infantry commander in Afghanistan, I retired from the military as an Army General, and Karla and I came right back here to Georgia to live on the farm. We are feeling very blessed. But talking with folks around here, a lot of people are not feeling so blessed. Veterans are struggling. The rising cost of living keeps everyone working extra hard to make ends meet. And everyday people just don't feel like anyone's listening to them.

Democrat.name: Katherine E. Juhan-Arnold
Democrat.bio: Katherine understands that agriculture isn't just about farmers — it's about families. It's about making sure food is safe, markets are fair, and rural communities have the support they need to thrive. It's about protecting consumers while also supporting the producers who feed them. And it's about recognizing that the Commissioner of Agriculture has a responsibility that goes far beyond ribbon cuttings and press releases.

Democrat.name: Sedrick Kent Rowe Jr.
Democrat.bio: From the football field at Fort Valley State to the research labs at Tuskegee University, Sedrick Rowe has always put in the work. As a first-generation farmer operating a 30-acre organic farm, Sedrick has been featured in The New York Times and advocates for young farmers nationwide. Now, he's ready to work for you.

Republican.name: Tyler J. Harper
Republican.bio: As a 7th generation farmer and small businessman who still uses his grandfather's tractor in the fields tending the same land that his family has had for over 100 years, Tyler Harper has a deep, personal appreciation for Georgia's agricultural industry - and the millions of local growers it supports. Since 2013, he has worked to defend Georgia's largest sector as a State Senator - where he currently serves in leadership on the Natural Resources, Public Safety, and Agriculture Committees. Additionally, Tyler currently serves as the Agriculture & Rural Development Vice-Chair of the Southern Legislative Conference. Over the years, he has championed our farms and farmers, worked to make them more competitive at home and abroad, and help cultivate the next generation of Georgia agriculture.  As your next Agriculture Commissioner, Tyler will ensure our food supplies are kept safe, making sure that our families are fed. He will always put Georgia agriculture first and fight the overreach of Washington, D.C.

races._note: Open seat — Chris Carr (R) vacated to run for Governor.

Republican.name: Robert Brian Strickland
Republican.bio: Brian Strickland grew up on a farm in Henry County, where his father still works the same land passed down from his great-grandfather. In high school, Brian watched his dad fight the Henry County Board of Education in a week-long jury trial after the government moved to take 40 acres of their family farm. That experience forged his lifetime commitment to the Republican values of property rights and the rule of law.

Republican.name: William Stone Cowsert
Republican.bio: Conservative Bill Cowsert has spent decades fighting for justice and protecting families. Raised in Macon and a graduate of the University of Georgia School of Law, Bill built his career as a tough, respected lawyer arguing hundreds of jury trials in countless courtrooms across Georgia.

Democrat.name: Bob Trammell
Democrat.bio: A former state legislator, county attorney, and longtime lawyer for families and small businesses, Bob has spent his career standing up for Georgians from all walks of life — both in the courtroom and at the Capitol. Bob believes justice shouldn't depend on your zip code, your bank account, or your connections; it is simply the right of each and every Georgian.

Democrat.name: Herbert Adams Jr.
Democrat.bio: Attorney.

Democrat.name: Tanya Miller
Democrat.bio: Tanya Miller is a respected trial attorney, a proven leader as Chair of the Georgia House Democratic Caucus and a fierce advocate for Georgians. Her career has been defined by one core principle: the law should serve people, not political interests.

races._note: Open seat — Brian Kemp (R) is term-limited and cannot seek re-election.

Democrat.name: Amanda Duffy
Democrat.bio: I've chosen to run for Governor of Georgia to give volume to the voices of the lower middle-class families like mine that are consistently stuck in financial survival mode. It does NOT have to be this hard.

Democrat.name: Derrick L. Jackson
Democrat.bio: Representative Jackson's journey began in the disciplined and demanding environment of the U.S. Navy, where he cultivated the leadership skills and work ethic that would later define his career in both the corporate and public sectors. Following his military service, he transitioned into a successful tenure at General Electric, where he spent nearly seven years as Senior Marketing Manager. In this role, he was not just a manager but a catalyst for growth, identifying $1 billion in global market opportunities and forging critical international strategic partnerships. These accomplishments stand as a testament to his ability to think strategically and act decisively.

Democrat.name: Geoffrey Lynn Duncan Jr.
Democrat.bio: Former Lieutenant Governor of Georgia (2019-2023). I'm running for governor as a proud Democrat because Georgians deserve leaders with the courage to take on Donald Trump and do what's right. I'll stand up to Trump and his allies here in Georgia and focus on what matters most to families—bringing down the cost of childcare, healthcare, and housing.

Democrat.name: Jason Fernando Esteves
Democrat.bio: Raised in Columbus, Georgia, Jason has always been driven by the values of hard work, perseverance, and leaving his community better than he found it. He grew up watching his father serve in the army and both of his parents work multiple jobs to make ends meet. Jason became the first in his family to go to college and eventually law school.

Democrat.name: Keisha R. Lance Bottoms
Democrat.bio: Keisha previously served as the 60th Mayor of Atlanta, where she steered the city through a pandemic, secured major investments in affordable housing, delivered a historic pay raise for Atlanta's first responders, became a national leader on civil rights reforms, supported small business and economic opportunity and balanced the budget. Keisha became the first Mayor in Atlanta's history to have served in all three branches of government, having previously served as a Judge and a City Councilmember.

Democrat.name: Michael "Mike" Thurmond
Democrat.bio: Served as Georgia's Labor Commissioner, where he transformed outdated unemployment offices into state-of-the-art career centers that facilitated the employment of over 300,000 Georgians. As Commissioner, Mike turned the unemployment office into a career center: created the Georgia Work$ model, that has been copied by more than 30 states.

Democrat.name: Olu Brown
Democrat.bio: Olu Brown has been a proud resident of Georgia for 25 years. A native of Lufkin, Texas, he graduated from Jarvis Christian College in Hawkins, Texas. In the early 2000s, driven by a calling to serve, he packed up his car and moved to Atlanta to pursue a Master of Divinity at Gammon Theological Seminary, part of the Interdenominational Theological Center.

Republican.name: Brad Raffensperger
Republican.bio: Current Secretary of State of Georgia. Brad Raffensperger is a man of faith and conservative businessman who stands on principle and puts hardworking Georgians first.

Republican.name: Burt Jones
Republican.bio: The 13th, and current, Lieutenant Governor of Georgia. Burt Jones is a business owner and job creator, 6th generation Georgia native, husband, father, former State Senator and the 13th Lieutenant Governor of the state of Georgia.

Republican.name: Christopher Michael Carr
Republican.bio: Current Attorney General of Georgia, has been in office since 2016 with a mission of protecting Georgians' lives, livelihoods and liberties.

Republican.name: Clark Headrick Dean
Republican.bio: Clark Dean isn't a career politician - he's a husband, a father, a devoted Christian, a respected business leader, and above all, a public servant. Clark has spent his life living the values that make Georgia strong: faith, family, service, and personal responsibility. As Governor, he will bring a servant's heart, a business leader's discipline, and a father's determination to fight for Georgia's future. Raised in a Christian household in the South, Clark learned early that faith is lived through service to others. Mission trips throughout his youth shaped his worldview and instilled a deep commitment to hard work, leadership, and community service.

Republican.name: Ken Yasger
Republican.bio: Ken Yasger has spent his life dedicated to service. At just fifteen years old he started his first job in the service industry. He joined the US Army and later the Georgia Army National Guard, dedicating over 12 years to serving our Country and State. Dedicated to enhancing the lives of Georgians, Ken has ran for public office twice. Ken's fight to win the Capital is more than a personal goal, Georgian's deserve fair, unbiased leadership that will put Georgian's first!

Republican.name: Rick Jackson
Republican.bio: Rick believes leadership isn't about speeches or political careers. It's about identifying problems, demanding results, and holding systems accountable. He believes Georgia needs an outsider — not a career politician — who will cut taxes, lower costs, secure the state, and make government work for the people. As Governor, Rick will fight to make Georgia the most affordable state in America by freezing property taxes, cutting the state income tax, eliminating wasteful spending, and raising standards in government and education.

Republican.name: Thomas E. Williams
Republican.bio: Tom is a true political outsider, who has never held public office. Ronald Reagan never held public office before running for and being elected Governor of California. Donald Trump never held public office before running for and being elected President of the United States. Tom is happy to follow in their footsteps.

Republican.name: John Franklin King
Republican.bio: Incumbent Insurance and Fire Safety Commissioner. As Georgia's Insurance and Safety Fire Commissioner, King has disrupted the status quo, put Georgia families first, and held the powerful accountable. He was appointed by Governor Brian Kemp in 2019 and elected to a full term in 2022, making him Georgia's first Hispanic statewide elected official.

Democrat.name: Ambuj "AJ" Jain
Democrat.bio: AJ Jain is running for Insurance Commissioner because he understands how the system is rigged against Georgia families — and he's done watching politicians let it happen. An immigrant who worked his way up surrounded by poverty, AJ built a successful career — first as an academic, then in business, and then starting a charity, Feed A Billion, which delivered more than 7 million meals to people in need.

Democrat.name: Clarence Blalock
Democrat.bio: The Office of Insurance Commissioner needs an independent advocate. Unfortunately, two of the last four commissioners are currently in prison, this is the consequence of letting big insurance into this vital oversight role. I hope we don't repeat that mistake in this primary.

Democrat.name: DeAndre Bernard Mathis
Democrat.bio: DeAndre Mathis isn't just a candidate; he's a public servant with a proven track record. From the Bowen Homes project on Atlanta's Westside to the deck of the USS Wisconsin during Operation Desert Storm, DeAndre has spent his life answering the call to serve.

Democrat.name: Keisha Sean Waites
Democrat.bio: Keisha Waites was elected in 2012 and represented the citizens of District 60 in the Georgia House of Representatives. District 60 includes Clayton County, Fulton County and is also home to the world's busiest airport, Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport and Porsche Global Headquarters. Keisha brings a broad record of civic involvement, strong business acumen, and fresh ideas to the role of public service.

Democrat.name: Brett A. Hulme
Democrat.bio: Brett was raised in a working-class family as the oldest of three boys, where he learned responsibility and work ethic. He started working young - delivering newspapers by bike, cutting grass, and later working weekends in a local sandwich shop - experiences that shaped his respect for people who work hard to provide for their families.

Democrat.name: Christian D. Smith
Democrat.bio: The real power to transform lives isn't in the courtroom—it's right here, in the Labor Commissioner's office. This is where we can give people pathways before they ever end up in the justice system. This is where we can address the affordability crisis that's crushing Georgia families. This is where we can make sure that if you work hard, you can afford a decent life.

Democrat.name: Clarence Blalock
Democrat.bio: Small business owner.

Democrat.name: Jason Matthew Moon
Democrat.bio: Jason Moon earned a bachelor's degree from Georgia Southern University in 2012 and an M.D. from the University of Georgia in 2018.

Democrat.name: Michelle "Michi" Sanchez
Democrat.bio: I want to build a Department of Labor that works for everyone it was created to serve. One that processes claims on time, holds bad actors accountable, expands job training and apprenticeships, and ensures that every worker in this state can go to work in safety and come home with their dignity intact. A department that is a resource for employers too, not just a place to file complaints, but a partner in building a stronger Georgia workforce.

Democrat.name: Nikki Porcher
Democrat.bio: She sees the people who are too often overlooked. Whether you're working a 9-to-5, holding down shifts, driving rideshare, caring for a family full-time, or piecing together jobs to get by, it's all work. And no matter what it looks like, you deserve a Labor Commissioner who will ensure that work works for you. Georgia is known as one of the best place for business. Nikki believes it's time for it also to be the best place to work, earn fair wages, and to build a future.

Republican.name: Bárbara Yolanda Rivera Holmes
Republican.bio: Incumbent Commissioner of Labor. From implementing cutting-edge strategies that streamline services to advancing workforce development and connecting Georgians to life-changing opportunities, I want you to know this - I will never stop fighting for you.Through innovation, partnership and relentless hard work, we will achieve real results that pave the way for a stronger, more prosperous Georgia — one that future generations will be proud to call home.

races._note: Open seat — Burt Jones (R) vacated to run for Governor.

Republican.name: Brenda Lynn Nelson-Porter
Republican.bio: Nelson-Porter's professional experience includes being a researcher. She has been affiliated with the International Standard Organization (ISO)-Research and Innovation Network, the American Association for the Advancement of Science, U.S. Black Chambers, Inc. (USBC), the National Association of Black Military Women (NABMW), the Journal of Women's Entrepreneurship and Education, the National Federation of Republican Women (NFRW), the Georgia Republican Assembly (GRA), and the Coweta County GOP (Newnan).

Republican.name: David Timothy Clark
Republican.bio: Serving in active combat with distinction in Afghanistan under Operation Enduring Freedom and later earning the title of Green Beret in the U.S. Army Special Forces. David currently serves parts of Gwinnett, Forsyth and Hall Counties in the Georgia House of Representatives, where he has served since 2015. David has served on key committees including chairing the Welfare Fraud Study Committee and Interstate Cooperation Committee. Today, he is ready to bring that same proven, principled leadership to the Lieutenant Governor's office.

Republican.name: Gregory Robert Dolezal
Republican.bio: Greg Dolezal grew up the son of a public school teacher and a paper salesman, spending summers on the family farm, learning the value of hard work and the occasional sunburn. Greg has founded and led businesses in manufacturing, health care, technology, and service industries while employing hundreds of Georgians along the way.

Republican.name: John Flanders Kennedy
Republican.bio: In the 2025 legislative session, I successfully worked alongside Governor Brian Kemp to author and champion his top legislative priority that reins in lawsuit abuse, protects consumers, and stands with our small businesses and job creators against powerful special interests. I haven't shied away from the tough fights in the state legislature—even when it meant taking on some in my own party. That's the kind of leadership I will bring to Georgia as our next Lt. Governor—one focused on conservative results, not rhetoric.

Republican.name: Michael Blake Tillery
Republican.bio: Blake is a lifelong conservative who believes in personal responsibility, constitutional rights, and protecting the next generation from the overreach of woke ideology. He's not afraid to take tough stands and follows through on his promises.

Republican.name: Steve Gooch
Republican.bio: Steve has been a consistent and vocal supporter of President Donald Trump ever since he came down the escalator in Trump Tower in 2015. The liberal Atlanta Journal-Constitution and other media outlets mockingly call him a MAGA champion at the State Capitol — and he wears that as a badge of honor. He proudly stands with President Trump and will continue fighting to restore the greatness of our country.

Republican.name: Takosha Misheal Swan
Republican.bio: Georgia deserves leadership that shows up, before, during, and after Election Day. Dr. Takosha Misheal Swan is running for Georgia Lieutenant Governor to restore access, accountability, and trust in state government. She will protect your voice, your family, and your money. As a combat veteran, former state public officer, Executive Board Member, Businesswoman, and mother, she understands leadership is not about titles, it is about service.

Democrat.name: Josh McLaurin
Democrat.bio: Josh is a state senator serving his fourth term in the state legislature, where our state's current Republican leadership bows down to Trump instead of standing up for Georgians. Josh is a Georgia native, an attorney, and a Double-Dawg graduate of the University of Georgia. He will stand up to Trump and MAGA bullies in Washington. It's time to refocus state government on working people and quality of life.

Democrat.name: Nabilah Parkes
Democrat.bio: In 2022, Senator Nabilah Parkes became the youngest woman ever elected to the Georgia State Senate, at 32 years old. She is also the first Muslim woman and the first South Asian woman elected to the Senate. In 2024, Senator Parkes made history again - becoming the first Georgia Senator to give birth while serving, strengthening her understanding of the challenges for new moms in the workplace. Senator Parkes lives in Duluth with her husband Bryan and their three children.

Democrat.name: Richard N. Wright
Democrat.bio: I'm not a career politician or a political insider—I'm a real leader who puts people first. My story is one of hard work, determination, and a commitment to serving the people of Georgia. My passion for public service and the desire to improve my community is what led me to run for public office.

Democrat.name: Peter Jacob Hubbard
Democrat.bio: Incumbent Public Service Commissioner, District 3. Using his 15 years of energy sector and market analysis experience, he argued against Georgia Power's flawed and overpriced plans and submitted alternative plans that would have kept utility bills low.

Republican.name: Brandon Lynn Martin
Republican.bio: The Public Service Commission is responsible for ensuring that Georgia has the infrastructure and energy generation capacity necessary to meet our needs now and into the future. Energy demand is increasing from both residential customers and businesses.  As your next PSC Commissioner, I'll make sure Georgia has access to safe, reliable & affordable energy so that Georgia can continue to be the best state to live, work and play.

Republican.name: Terrell Fitz Johnson Sr.
Republican.bio: As a 21-year veteran of the U.S. Army and a successful businessman, Fitz Johnson knows a little something about winning tough fights. When liberals in Washington tried to force their woke Green New Deal on Georgia and cost our families and crush our businesses, Fitz stood up for ratepayers and stopped their agenda at the state line. Now, Fitz is championing an America First energy agenda that delivers affordability and reliability for every Georgia ratepayer.

races._note: Open seat — Tricia Pridemore (R) did not file for re-election; she is running for U.S. House District 11.

Republican.name: Bobby Mehan
Republican.bio: Robert "Bobby" Mehan is a Georgia small business owner, entrepreneur, and Republican candidate for the Georgia Public Service Commission (PSC) who believes everyday ratepayers deserve a strong voice when decisions are made about electricity rates, power bills, and energy policy in Georgia. Bobby is a third generation Georgian who has lived in rural West Georgia for nearly two decades and spent his career building businesses, creating jobs, and serving customers across the state.

Republican.name: Carolyn Tatum Roddy
Republican.bio: Carolyn Roddy earned a high school diploma from Habersham Central High School. She attended North Georgia College, earned a bachelor's degree from the University of Georgia in 1975, and a law degree from the University of Georgia in 1978. Her career experience includes working as an attorney at law.

Republican.name: Joshua Aaron Tolbert
Republican.bio: I'm not a politician. I'm an engineer, educator, expert witness, and small business owner with deep experience in the industries the PSC oversees — including nuclear power, coal, oil and gas, biomass, geothermal, and renewables, as well as major industrial facilities like pulp and paper mills, steel plants, chemical facilities, and manufacturing sites. Georgia needs technical competence at the PSC. That has been the core of my entire career.

Democrat.name: Angelia Pressley
Democrat.bio: I decided to enter this race after attending PSC hearings and witnessing citizens desperate to be heard. I saw commissioners ignore families struggling to pay their bills while prioritizing monopoly profits. I am running to be the voice for fairness and common sense that is currently missing in our state leadership.

Democrat.name: Craig L. Cupid
Democrat.bio: Craig is a man of faith, a dedicated husband, and a proud father of two growing boys. Before becoming an attorney, he earned his electrical engineering degree from Georgia Tech, later completing his law degree at Georgia State University College of Law. This path has shaped Craig's practical, problem-solving approach to service.

Democrat.name: Shelia Edwards
Democrat.bio: Shelia brings a strong record of leadership, advocacy, and proven campaign experience, with work spanning the local, state, and federal levels. She has served in senior leadership roles within Georgia's Democratic infrastructure - helping to strengthen and expand the Democratic message while delivering real results for communities across the state. Shelia will put “People Before Profits” and give power back to Georgia ratepayers

races._note: Open seat — Brad Raffensperger (R) vacated to run for Governor.

Democrat.name: Adrian Consonery Jr.
Democrat.bio: I am the product of two entrepreneurs who built their lives through honesty, hard work, and determination. My father, a mechanic, and my mother, a counselor, built a home centered on one core value, helping people fix what's broken. That foundation shaped my passion for service, innovation, and community outreach, values that continue to guide my work today.

Democrat.name: Cam Thi Ashling
Democrat.bio: She's a small business owner, former licensed stockbroker, livestock farmer, and single mother who understands how Georgia's systems actually work — and how they can make or break working families and small businesses. Her family came to Gwinnett County as political refugees from Vietnam, arriving with a single aluminum trunk and the determination to build a better future. Her parents worked factory and warehouse jobs for decades — her father as a forklift driver before wiring commercial HVAC systems, and her mother working night shifts in manufacturing — sacrificing everything to create opportunity for their daughters.

Democrat.name: Dana Barrett
Democrat.bio: Dana Barrett is currently serving as the Fulton County Commissioner for District 3. She is also a small business owner, former tech executive, an award-winning TV, radio, & podcast host, and the 2020 Democratic Nominee for US Congress for Georgia's 11th District.

Democrat.name: Penny Brown Reynolds
Democrat.bio: She believes this office must stand as the guardian of fairness and opportunity. From protecting the right to vote, to empowering small business, modernizing professional licenses, safeguarding seniors, and ensuring government accountability - she brings extensive experience in executive leadership and management, vision, and a heart to serve. This pivotal moment in Georgia calls for courage. It calls for integrity. And it calls for someone who knows what it means to rise from humble beginnings—and still reach back to lift others. She is someone who knows how to make government work for the people and get the job done.

Republican.name: Edward "Ted" Metz
Republican.bio: I support: Voter ID, Absentee Signature Matching, Hand Marked Paper Ballots, Strict Chain of Custody, Comprehensive Audits, Public Ballot Inspection, Prosecution, Penalties, and Imprisonment for Unlawful Election Shenanigans. I DO NOT support: Electronic Voting Systems, Ballot Drop Boxes, Ballot Harvesting, Ballot Stuffing, Private and Corporate Election Funding, 3rd Party Temporary Election Staffing

Republican.name: Kelvin D. King
Republican.bio: Kelvin spent most of his childhood in a single-parent home and they oftentimes struggled to make ends meet. Though he became the man of the family early, his mother and grandmother worked hard to instill in him the values needed to grow into a successful young adult. It was these values he credits with earning him an appointment to the United States Air Force Academy where he was an All-Conference football player. Kelvin graduated from the Academy and served 5 years in the Air Force as a Contracting Officer. He made the rank of Captain before deciding to leave the military and transition to corporate America. Upon completion of his service, he returned to Georgia to begin building his life as a civilian.

Republican.name: Robert Gabriel Sterling
Republican.bio: Conservative. Ready. Real

Republican.name: Timothy Kyle Fleming
Republican.bio: Tim Fleming is a proven conservative, small business owner, and a champion for election integrity with a strong record of fighting for Georgia. As former Chief of Staff to Gov. Kemp and Deputy Secretary of State under then Sec. Kemp, Tim took on Stacey Abrams' election schemes, cracked down on voter fraud, and streamlined professional licensing. Now serving in the State House, he continues to lead efforts to secure our elections and cut red tape. As Secretary of State, Tim will keep fighting to protect our elections, simplify licensing, and defend Georgia values.

Republican.name: Vernon Jones
Republican.bio: Vernon is fighting for Georgia's future: defending families, protecting communities, and keeping Georgia the best state in America to live, work, and do business. Now, he's running for Secretary of State to restore integrity, trust, and transparency to Georgia's elections — and put the people, not the politicians, back in charge.

Republican.name: Fred "Bubba" Longgrear
Republican.bio: Bubba is running for State School Superintendent because he believes Georgia public schools are facing a leadership crisis. His vision is clear: raise literacy levels, improve school safety, expand career pathways, and restore trust between the Department of Education, local districts, teachers, parents, and families and build a culture of investment in the next generation.

Republican.name: Mesha Mainor
Republican.bio: Mesha is running for Georgia State Superintendent of Schools because she believes every child deserves access to a high-quality education, regardless of their background or zip code. She'll bring transparency, accountability, and bold leadership to Georgia's education, expanding school choice, strengthening career and technical education, and ensuring schools are safe, focused on academics, and free from political agendas. If Georgia is the #1 state to do business, we must also be #1 in education.

Republican.name: Nelva Melissa Lee
Republican.bio: Dr. Lee was a Governor Kemp appointed board member to the Department of Community Health. She is the Vice Chairman of the board of the Henry County Pregnancy Resource Center (Bridge Wellness South), a former Republican candidate for State Senate in District 17, and current candidate for Georgia Superintendent of Schools. Dr. Lee has authored several Christian-based books including: “Stay and Fight” and “Be the Super Hero of Your Story.

Republican.name: Randell Eugene Trammell
Republican.bio: I am deeply passionate about the power of education to transform lives and communities. My work in youth civic development is driven by a vision of a more informed, engaged, and empowered generation of young leaders who will shape the future of our society.

Republican.name: Richard Lee Woods
Republican.bio: Incumbent State School Superintendent.

Democrat.name: Anton Anthony
Democrat.bio: Over the course of his career, Dr. Anthony has served as a classroom teacher in Burke County, assistant principal in Muscogee County and McDuffie County, principal in Sumter County and Richmond County, Human Resources Manager in DeKalb County, and currently serves as Superintendent of Hancock County Schools in Sparta, Georgia. He is the only candidate who has served students across rural, suburban, and urban communities in Georgia. For Dr. Anthony, this race is not a career move. It is a calling.

Democrat.name: Lydia Catalina Powell
Democrat.bio: My career includes 11 years in educational leadership, where I have worked collaboratively with educators, families, and community partners to support high-quality learning environments. I have served as a teacher in Lee (Florida) Dekalb, Henry, Clayton, and Fayette County Schools. I have served in alternative, high achievement, and low income schools.

Democrat.name: Otha Thornton
Democrat.bio: The role of State Superintendent demands an executive who can navigate policy, manage a vast budget, and secure resources. Otha has done this at a national level. As the elected National President of the Parent Teacher Association, he provided executive leadership for one of the nation's most complex non-profits—managing a multi-million-dollar budget and a network of over 4 million members. At state level, Otha has served as the Georgia PTA's Legislative Chair and has advised chairs following his tenure. Currently, he maintains his decades of advocacy in the advising of State Legislators on education and child advocacy matters. This experience is direct preparation for uniting Georgia's various school districts and ensuring state policy works for every community.

Republican.name: Michael David Cameron
Republican.bio: In 2020 Mike was elected as State Representative for Georgia House District 1, which includes parts of Walker and all of Dade Counties. In 2022 and 2024, Mike was reelected as your State Representative.

Democrat.name: Bentley Hudgins
Democrat.occupation: Georgia State Director Human Rights Campaign

Democrat.name: Alana Sanders
Democrat.bio: I have served as a local elected official where I have written resolutions and championed community priorities. As a lobbyist for the State of Georgia, I have written bills and testified on various issues affecting our communities. My work with Newton County Commission demonstrated my commitment to delivering results and understanding how to navigate the legislative process effectively. I believe in concrete action and will consistently report on the progress and outcomes of my efforts, ensuring transparency and trust.

Democrat.name: Karla Daniels Hooper
Democrat.bio: As your State Representative, I will be a progressive voice in the Georgia State House and a present, accountable partner here at home. I will work to lower the cost of living, expand access to quality and affordable healthcare, strengthen our public schools, and hold powerful corporations accountable when they put profits over people. You will see me at the grocery store, the park, church, neighborhood meetings, and local cookouts; listening, learning, and serving. Representation does not stop in Atlanta; it begins and ends in the community. In the spirit of my father's legacy, and guided by the values he passed down to me, I am proud to announce my candidacy for the Georgia House of Representatives in District 113.

Democrat.name: Tiffany N Walker
Democrat.occupation: Founder of Smu Cosmetology and Business College

Democrat.name: Tony Michael Brown
Democrat.occupation: Former Lieutenant Governor Candidate & Air Force Veteran & Military Officer & Dhs

Democrat.name: Holle Weiss-Friedman
Democrat.occupation: Retired Trial Lawyer, Municipal Judge Pro-Tem & Mediator

Democrat.name: Sylvester Smith Iii
Democrat.occupation: Business Intelligence Specialist and Small Business Owner

Republican.name: Gregory Pop Thomas
Republican.occupation: 25 Yr Air Force Veteran, (telecommunication) Double Major: Ms Information Systems and Ma Theology
